en:
My wife had an appointment with the dentist this afternoon, but she was terrified of going alone.
I had to drive her to the clinic and wait in the garden while she saw the doctor.
She trembled so much in the waiting room that the nurse felt sorry for her.
When her name was called, I went outside for a cigarette and sat on a garden seat.
Suddenly she rushed out of the back door, having remembered leaving her wallet beside me.
At the same moment, I knocked on the dentist's window to ask for a light.
"It's only me!" I exclaimed, but my wife and the dentist were both astonished.
I had entered the wrong garden and frightened everyone for no urgent reason at all.
cn:
我妻子今天下午预约了看牙医，可她害怕独自前往。
我不得不开车送她去诊所，在她看牙时在花园里等着。
她在候诊室里紧张得直发抖，护士都替她难过。
叫到她的名字时，我到外面抽烟，坐在花园长椅上。
她突然从后门冲出来——想起把钱包落在我旁边了。
与此同时，我敲了敲牙医的窗户想借个火。
「是我！」我大声说，可妻子和牙医都吓了一跳。
我进错了花园，毫无紧急理由却把大家都吓坏了。

en:
Our neighbour Hurstwood is a huge man who drives an old truck and wears rough clothes.
To strangers he looks poor, but those who know him tell a different story.
He used to be the most powerful gangster in the district and once controlled a large territory.
Though he has retired from that life, he is used to living in great luxury.
His apartment is filled with fine paintings and bottles of expensive wine.
He says a man must keep his own standards, however odd they may seem.
The police no longer watch him, yet his past has never been forgotten.
Hurstwood remains a noble figure in his own strange way.
cn:
我们的邻居赫斯特伍德是个大块头，开着旧卡车、穿着粗布衣服。
陌生人觉得他穷，但了解他的人说法不同。
他过去是这一带最有势力的匪徒，曾控制着一大片地盘。
虽已金盆洗手，他却习惯于奢侈的生活。
他的公寓里摆满名画和昂贵的红酒。
他说，人总得有自己的标准，不管看起来多古怪。
警察不再监视他，但他的过去从未被人遗忘。
赫斯特伍德仍以自己奇特的方式像个「贵族」。

en:
A small boy went into a sweet shop and chose a large bar of chocolate.
As he paid, a fifty-pence piece slipped out of his hand and fell into a narrow crack.
He lay flat on the floor and stretched his arm into the gap, but it was not wide enough.
The shop assistant grew impatient and telephoned the police.
When they arrived, the boy still refused to leave without his money.
He said the coin was all the property he possessed in the world.
The policeman had to pry open the gap with a tool and return the coin.
He then scolded the boy for causing so much trouble over fifty pence.
cn:
一个小男孩走进糖果店，挑了一大块巧克力。
付钱时，一枚五十便士硬币从手中滑落，掉进一道狭缝里。
他趴在地上把胳膊伸进缝隙，但缝不够宽，够不着。
店员不耐烦了，打电话报了警。
警察赶到时，男孩仍不肯不要钱就走。
他说那枚硬币是他在这世上仅有的财产。
警察只好用工具撬开缝隙，把硬币还给他。
随后训斥他为五十便士惹了这么多麻烦。

en:
Mary, the daughter of a farmer, had a little lamb that she treated as a pet.
The lamb was accustomed to following her wherever she went on the farm.
One morning it followed her all the way to school and into the classroom.
The children were delighted, but the teacher was obliged to lead it outside.
From then on, the lamb waited at the school gate until Mary came out.
The headmaster finally permitted it to stay on the grass, on one condition.
Mary had to tend the animal during breaks and accept full responsibility.
It became the most famous companion in the history of the little school.
cn:
农民的女儿玛丽养了一头小羔羊当宠物。
羔羊习惯了在农场里到处跟着她。
一天早上它一路跟到学校，还进了教室。
孩子们高兴坏了，老师却不得不把它领出去。
此后羔羊每天在校门口等玛丽放学。
校长最终允许它待在草地上，但有一个条件。
玛丽必须在课间照料它，并承担全部责任。
它成了这所小学校史上最出名的伙伴。

en:
The Verrazano-Narrows Bridge links Staten Island with Brooklyn across a wide channel.
When it was completed, its main span of 4,260 feet was the longest in the world.
Four enormous cables, each made of tens of thousands of steel wires, support the deck.
Engineers had to estimate the immensity of the forces acting on such a length.
The towers rise high above the water, built of steel and concrete.
Since 1964 the bridge has carried a huge daily capacity of traffic.
It has become one of the most recognizable features of the New York skyline.
For decades it has proved that human skill can master even the widest spans.
cn:
韦拉扎诺海峡大桥横跨宽阔水道，连接斯塔滕岛与布鲁克林。
竣工时，其四二千二百六十英尺的主跨是世界上最长的。
四根巨型钢缆——每根由数万根钢丝绞成——悬吊着桥面。
工程师必须估算如此长度上所承受的巨大力量。
桥塔由钢和混凝土建成，高耸于水面之上。
自一九六四年以来，大桥每天承载海量车流。
它已成为纽约天际线最易辨认的地标之一。
数十年来，它证明了人的技艺足以征服最宽的跨度。

en:
A modern art gallery opened an unusual exhibition of wire sculptures and electric lights.
The works belonged to a young artist who believed that current could be part of art.
On the opening night, a student helped to connect the pieces to the mains.
He joined the wrong circuit, and a spark set fire to material near one sculpture.
Several exhibits were damaged before the fire brigade brought the blaze under control.
The owner said the gallery possessed only a few such valuable pieces.
The student argued that risk belongs to every new form of expression.
The burnt corner of the room has been remembered as part of the story ever since.
cn:
一家现代艺术画廊举办了电线雕塑与电灯组成的奇特展览。
作品属于一位年轻艺术家——他认为电流也可以是艺术的一部分。
开幕当晚，一名学生帮忙把展品接入电源。
他接错了线路，火花引燃了某件雕塑旁的材料。
消防队控制住火势前，几件展品已受损。
画廊老板说馆内这样的珍品所剩无几。
学生辩称，风险属于每一种新的表达形式。
房间被熏黑的一角，从此成了这段故事的一部分。

en:
Mrs Smith owned a valuable Persian cat with a famous pedigree.
One evening the animal disappeared, and she was distraught.
Two days later she received an anonymous call demanding a large ransom.
The voice warned that she had better not inform the police.
Reluctant but desperate, she left the money at the place she had been told.
That night the cat was returned, safe and unharmed.
She later discovered that thieves had ransacked the house before taking it.
She had better put an address on its collar, she said, or face the same astoundingly dear trouble again.
cn:
史密斯太太养了一只血统名贵的波斯猫。
一天傍晚猫不见了，她心急如焚。
两天后她接到匿名电话，索要高额赎金。
对方警告她最好不要报警。
她虽不情愿，还是按指示把钱送到了指定地点。
当晚猫被送还，安然无恙。
后来她发现窃贼偷猫前把屋子翻了个遍。
她说，最好给猫戴上地址项圈，否则还会碰上同样「贵重」的麻烦。

en:
At the beginning of the twentieth century, aviation was still in its infancy.
Santos-Dumont, a Brazilian pioneer, designed both balloons and powered aircraft.
In Paris he was able to fly a small airship around the Eiffel Tower.
In 1906 he was able to make the first recorded powered flight in Europe.
Earlier attempts by others had ended after only a few seconds in the air.
Few pilots could control such light machines in a strong wind.
He set records that astonished the public and encouraged further experiments.
His courage helped to prove that sustained flight was no longer a dream.
cn:
二十世纪初，航空业尚处襁褓。
巴西先驱桑托斯·杜蒙特既设计气球，也设计动力飞行器。
在巴黎，他成功驾驶小飞艇绕埃菲尔铁塔飞行。
一九〇六年，他完成了欧洲有记录以来首次动力飞行。
更早的尝试者大多只能在空中停留几秒钟。
很少有飞行员能在强风中操控如此轻的机器。
他创造的纪录令公众震惊，也鼓舞了更多试验。
他的勇气帮助证明：持续飞行已不再是梦。

en:
Daniel Mendoza was born in London in 1764 and became a famous boxer.
He rose from poverty to fame through his agile style and scientific method.
He was admired by rich and poor alike and defeated many powerful opponents.
Later he opened a boxing school and was well paid for teaching the wealthy.
However, he spent money freely and was imprisoned more than once for debt.
His last years were marked by hardship, though his name was never forgotten.
He has been remembered as the man who brought honour to the sport.
His story is still told as a lesson in talent, fame and ruin.
cn:
丹尼尔·门多萨一七六四年出生于伦敦，后成为著名拳击手。
他凭敏捷的打法和「科学」风格，从贫寒走向名声。
无论贫富，人们都很仰慕他；他击败过许多强敌。
后来他开办拳击学校，教富人子弟，收入颇丰。
但他挥霍无度，因欠债多次入狱。
晚年困苦不堪，但姓名从未被遗忘。
人们铭记他是为这项运动赢得声誉的人。
他的故事至今仍被当作关于天赋、名声与沉沦的教训来讲述。

en:
The actor had been given a long part in a new play and was told to learn it by heart.
He rehearsed for weeks and was confident that he knew every line.
On opening night he walked on stage unaware that two scenes were in the wrong order.
He opened his mouth and suddenly could not remember what came next.
The audience waited in silence while he stood in embarrassment.
After a few seconds he began to improvise, and somehow finished the act.
The director later said it was hard to tell memorizing from understanding.
From that night on, he never confused learning by heart with knowing a role.
cn:
这位演员在新戏里分到一段很长的角色，被告知必须背熟台词。
他排练了好几周，确信自己句句烂熟。
首演之夜他走上台，却没意识到两场戏的顺序记反了。
他一开口，忽然想不起下一句是什么。
观众静静等着，他在台上尴尬地站着。
几秒后他开始即兴发挥，总算把戏演完了。
导演后来说，死记硬背与真正理解很难区分。
从那一晚起，他再也不把背台词和懂角色混为一谈。

en:
A television programme once invited ordinary people to tell their most unusual stories.
Viewers voted for the best tale, and for a time the show appealed to millions.
No matter how strange the stories were, audiences could not get enough of them.
Soon similar programmes flooded every channel, and the format began to tire people.
What had been one man's meat became another man's poison overnight.
Producers tried every variety of entertainment, but no single show pleased everyone.
No matter what they offered, some viewers always switched to something else.
The industry learned that taste is personal and fame can disappear very quickly.
cn:
有一档电视节目曾请普通人讲述自己最离奇的经历。
观众投票选出最佳故事，一度吸引了数百万人。
无论故事多离奇，观众都看不厌。
很快同类节目挤满各频道，这种套路开始让人厌倦。
一个人的佳肴，一夜之间成了另一个人的毒药。
制作人尝试了各种花样，却没有一档节目能讨好所有人。
无论他们推出什么，总有观众换台。
这个行业懂得了：品味因人而异，名声也可能转瞬即逝。

en:
A man went out in his slippers to empty the dustbin, and the wind shut the front door.
He was locked outside without a key, wearing only a thin coat.
His neighbours were away, so he went to the pub and telephoned the fire brigade.
The firemen put a ladder against the wall and helped him in through a window.
When his wife came home, she said he needn't have called them at all.
She had a spare key and would have returned within an hour.
He admitted it was a rash decision made out of embarrassment.
It was only a spot of bother, but an expensive one.
cn:
一位先生穿着拖鞋出去倒垃圾，风把前门带上了。
他没带钥匙，只穿着一件薄外套，被锁在了门外。
邻居都不在，他去了酒吧，打电话叫消防队。
消防员架起梯子，从窗户把他弄进屋里。
妻子回家时说他根本不必叫他们。
她有备用钥匙，一个小时内就会回来。
他承认那是出于尴尬做出的鲁莽决定。
只是一点儿小麻烦，却花了不少冤枉钱。
